title: ufputc
text: ufputc Copy additional data from a program buffer into a Uniface field. Explain this code short ufputc (unsigned char* field,unsigned char* buffer,short length); Copy Parameters field The name of a field in your component. To resolve ambiguity, you can qualify the field name with an entity name, for example, field . entity . Instead of the actual field name, you also use the internal bind name as returned by ugetbind . buffer The data to be copied to the field. This data does not have to be zero-terminated. length The number of bytes that should be copied from buffer to field . Use a negative value to indicate that more data is to follow (using additional calls to ufputc ). Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ufput, ufputi, ufputc, and ufputext Value Meaning -1 An error occurred. 0 All data was copied to the field . 1 There is more data to be copied to the field. Occurs when you have specified a negative Length value. Use Library: yrtl Description ufputc moves an additional block of data from a program buffer to a Uniface field. Call this function only after a previous call to ufput or ufputi with a negative length. Parameters and return values are the same as for ufput . If you call ufputc with a negative length, you must call it repeatedly until you use a non-negative length . For more information, see the "ufput" topic. Example: ufput, ufputc, ufputi, and ugetbind The following example reads a graphics file from disk in chunks of 5000 bytes, and transfers the contents to the Uniface image field IMGFIELD. This field should be defined in the form as: Data Type—Image (database) Widget Type—Picture Interface—R* Note that, in the calls to ufput and ufputc , we specify a negative length, because we are not sure if there is more data until we have read the entire file. Once we have read all the file, we issue a final call to ufputc with length zero to denote that the transfer is complete. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> EXPORT void GETIMAGE () { FILE* fp; short len; char buf[5000]; char bindname[80]; /* Use bindname for efficiency */ ugetbind("IMGFIELD", bindname); /* Open binary read mode */ fp = fopen("d:\\temp\\mypicture.jpg", "rb"); len = fread(buf, 1, 5000, fp); /* Read first chunk */ ufput(bindname, buf, -len); /* Assume more to come */ while (len > 0) { len = fread(buf, 1, 5000, fp); /* Read next chunk */ if ( len > 0 ) /* Assume more to come */ ufputc(bindname, buf, -len); } ufputc(bindname, buf, 0); /* Tell Uniface it's done */ fclose(fp); /* and close file */ } Copy Related Links ufput Copy data from a program buffer into a Uniface field.
